message:
fix: SSE buffer limits, TUI markup parser, extension event serialization, and misc hardening

SSE parser safety (sse.rs):
- Add 100MB per-event data limit to prevent OOM from malicious or
  broken streams that send unlimited data: fields in a single event.
- Add 10MB total buffer limit with automatic state reset and error
  event emission on overflow.
- Fix incomplete UTF-8 at EOF: clear parser state on terminal error
  instead of emitting infinite error events.
- Add test_buffer_limit_overflow_resets_parser_state test.

TUI markup parser (tui.rs):
- Rewrite strip_markup() bracket-tag detection to use explicit state
  machine instead of iterator peeking.
- Correctly handle edge cases: [0] (pure digits = not a tag), empty
  brackets [] (not a tag), and filter invalid markup chars.

Extension events (extension_events.rs):
- Add #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] to all ExtensionEvent variants
  so field names serialize as camelCase (sessionId, sessionFile, toolName,
  toolCallId, turnIndex, etc.) matching the TypeScript event wire format.

Doctor diagnostics (doctor.rs):
- Add rg and fd/fdfind to shell tool checks (Severity::Warn) since
  grep and find tools depend on them at runtime.
- Recognize additional config keys: checkForUpdates, sessionDurability,
  markdown, queueMode (both camelCase and snake_case forms).

Config (config.rs):
- Add warning log when an unknown extension policy profile string is
  encountered, helping operators diagnose misconfigurations.

Main (main.rs):
- Move theme validation after --version check so the ultra-fast
  --version path doesn't waste time validating themes.
- Session index maintenance thread now always spawns for background
  cleanup_temp_files() even when no reindex is needed.

Share (interactive/share.rs):
- Fix parse_gist_url_and_id() to require exactly 2 path segments
  (owner/gist-id), rejecting profile URLs (/owner) and non-canonical
  paths (/owner/id/raw) as false positives.
- Add tests for profile link and non-canonical path rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
